About Me
🎨
Design + Code Where creativity meets craft

Design that
solves, not
just delights.

Over the past decade, I've built and scaled design teams, led cross-functional initiatives, and delivered digital experiences across fintech, SaaS, eCommerce, and emerging markets — turning complex challenges into simple, elegant, and measurable solutions.

I'm also a front-end developer, which means I don't just design — I build. My work lives at the intersection of thoughtful design and clean, production-ready code.

I believe the foundations of great design lie in communication — asking the right questions, challenging assumptions, and collaborating relentlessly. Every obstacle is fuel for better work.

Product Strategy Design Systems React / Next.js Figma Team Leadership UX Research Prototyping Accessibility

worked.

July 2024 — Present Current
Product Designer (UI/UX)
Niteon
Gathering and evaluating user requirements in close collaboration with product managers and engineers. Designing end-to-end interface elements — menus, navigation, widgets, and interactive prototypes — while preparing and presenting concepts to internal teams and senior stakeholders. Also supports the CEO with fundraising data, venture insights, and strategic oversight.
Jan 2024 — May 2025
Product Designer (UI/UX)
NEDSOFT HRMS
Led user research, usability testing, and iterative design for a B2B HR platform — translating findings into personas, journey maps, wireframes, and high-fidelity prototypes. Developed comprehensive design systems and worked cross-functionally with product managers and developers to deliver intuitive, accessible, and well-documented HR solutions.
Oct 2022 — Jan 2025
Graphic Manager · Product Designer · Social Media Manager
Marvel Natural PLC, Ikeja, Lagos
Led a design team in crafting product designs that merged functionality with aesthetic appeal. Oversaw production of marketing materials — print, digital, and social — while developing and maintaining brand guidelines to ensure visual consistency. Managed project timelines and budgets, and championed user-centered design across all output.
